claim: The /context command in Claude Code shows all tools being loaded and their token cost; disabling unused MCP tools saves significant context window and improves speed.
actionable: Run /context regularly, especially in long sessions; identify tools you aren't using (e.g., Zapier integrations you don't need) and disable them per-session to preserve context.
source_title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide | MCP, Skills & More

claim: Skills in Claude Code are wrapper behaviors defined in markdown files that Claude learns and can invoke repeatedly; they allow consistent formatting and methodology without re-prompting.
actionable: When you do a task multiple times (code review, deployment, testing), wrap it in a skill: ask Claude to create a markdown file with step-by-step process, then invoke it via /[skill-name] command.
source_title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide | MCP, Skills & More

claim: Claude Code automatically creates slash commands for custom skills; once a skill.md is in the correct folder, you can invoke it with /skill-name or Claude will auto-invoke it when detecting relevant context.
actionable: Create skills in .claude/skills/[skill-name]/skill.md; test invocation with /[skill-name]; enable auto-invocation by designing skill names that match common task language.
source_title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide | MCP, Skills & More

claim: GitHub MCP server setup requires a personal access token (PAT) and should be scoped globally via --scope user flag so it's available across all projects, not just the current one.
actionable: Generate a GitHub PAT with repo access; run: claude mcp add json github [PAT] --scope user; verify with /mcp and viewing GitHub in the list.
source_title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide | MCP, Skills & More

claim: Context7 plugin provides live, up-to-date library documentation; it's free and accessible via Claude desktop app's plugins section, dramatically improving Claude's ability to reference correct syntax.
actionable: Install Context7 plugin from Claude desktop app; use it when asking Claude about library APIs to ensure you get current documentation instead of training-data approximations.
source_title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide | MCP, Skills & More

claim: Sub-agents can be auto-created by Claude or manually configured; auto-created ones are often better because Claude designs them task-specifically, whereas manual configuration rarely improves on automatic generation.
actionable: Instead of pre-building custom sub-agents, let Claude auto-spawn them as needed for parallelization; only manually create sub-agents if you need very specific tool restrictions (e.g., no deletion tools).
source_title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide | MCP, Skills & More

claim: Claude Code has no persistent memory by default across sessions; all long-term context must be stored in claude.md files that are auto-loaded at session start.
actionable: Create and maintain a claude.md file (or intelligent folder structure) with project info, decisions, preferences; reference it in session prompts so Claude has persistent context across restarts.
source_title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide | MCP, Skills & More

claim: Shift+Tab in Claude Code toggles between 'accept edits plan mode' (asks for approval each edit) and 'auto-accept edits'; use auto-accept for trusted routines, plan mode for experimental work.
actionable: Press Shift+Tab to toggle edit mode; switch to auto-accept for high-confidence tasks (cleanup, refactoring with tests), stay in plan mode for new features or risky changes.
source_title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide | MCP, Skills & More

claim: Nimbalyst (open-source, free visual editor for Claude Code) provides a Kanban board view of all active sessions, markdown viewer with diffs, and custom extensions; it doesn't replace Claude Code but enhances visibility.
actionable: Install Nimbalyst if managing multiple Claude Code sessions; use its Kanban view to track progress across parallel tasks and custom extensions to build project-specific dashboards.
source_title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide | MCP, Skills & More

claim: Playwright plugin enables Claude Code to auto-test websites by spinning up browser instances; requires explicit setup but dramatically improves confidence in web app changes.
actionable: Install Playwright plugin; ask Claude to write Playwright test scripts that verify your web changes automatically, reducing manual testing overhead.
source_title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide | MCP, Skills & More
